data.body:
G4 container child-consistency audit (parent f89d-e54d-203d-4ee0 √ó THIS child 822a "Honest bidirectional round-trip probe; de-encode 'expected-bug' guards"), all 7 cross-child interaction modes checked against the WHOLE sibling roster (2359/33d0/59ef/8937/fe57) under the high-confidence / benign-reading filter, corroborated against the repo. VERDICT: clean pairing ‚Äî no G4 cross-child finding.

Repo corroboration of 822a's premises and surfaces:
- The probe guard 822a de-encodes exists verbatim: scripts/jira-pressure-test/e2e_field_validation_probe.sh L1542-1547 ‚Äî `fail_test "Phase3.verify-inbound-comments-gap" "unexpectedly synced inbound"` + `matrix_set "comments" "inbound" "update" "UNEXPECTED_PASS"` on the SYNCED branch, and `pass_test "...inbound NOT implemented, as expected"` + `matrix_set "comments" "inbound" "update" "NOT-SYNCED"` (L1547) on the not-synced branch. This is exactly the "encodes a bug as expected / would FAIL if it synced" inversion 822a AC2 converts. A repo search for `issuelinks` under scripts/jira-pressure-test returned no matches, corroborating 822a's premise "no probe round-trips relationships at all."
- The bidirectional behavior 822a's hermetic round-trip asserts is ALREADY present in production (the epic closes a TEST/PROBE-coverage seam, not re-fixes the bugs): inbound `comment` key read + `issuelinks` enrichment + outbound batch link dispatch all exist. 822a's comment/link/parent round-trip is therefore assertable against the CURRENT production path without an unmet sibling-delivered fix.

7-mode walk for THIS pairing:
- Implicit shared state ‚Äî BENIGN. 822a file_impact = scripts/jira-pressure-test/e2e_field_validation_probe.sh (de-encode guards) + tests/integration/rebar_reconciler/test_roundtrip.py (new). No sibling touches the probe script or test_roundtrip.py; the outbound-applier cluster (33d0‚Üíapply_outbound.py/batch_dispatch.py/applier.py; 2359‚Üíbatch_dispatch.py/applier.py/reconcile.py) and the schema/fixture/contract files (8937‚Üí_snapshot_schema.py/test_snapshot_contract.py/fetcher.py; fe57‚Üí_fakes.py/fixtures/capture script; 59ef‚Üítest_acliclient_contract.py/acli_graph.py) are all disjoint from 822a's edits. No shared-file co-edit.
- Conflicting assumptions ‚Äî BENIGN. 822a assumes comments/links/parent round-trip "through the production path"; repo confirms inbound `comment`/`issuelinks` reads and outbound batch link dispatch already exist, consistent with fe57 (fixtures flow through fetch_snapshot unmassaged), 8937 (snapshot-entry schema), and 33d0 (production batch applier). 822a's round-trip asserts inbound‚Üîoutbound OUTCOMES, not the outbound `labels_applied` sub-op payload representation ‚Äî so the labels list-vs-count mismatch tracked on 2359/33d0 is outbound-telemetry-internal and untouched by 822a.
- Dependency gap ‚Äî BENIGN. 822a's only input dependency ‚Äî story A's fixtures/fake ‚Äî is correctly declared via depends_on fe57. The producer‚Üîconsumer schema/contract (8937) is explicitly scoped OUT and is not required to stand up a behavioral round-trip. The bug fixes its round-trip exercises are already in code, so 822a has no unmet "fix-must-land-first" edge to a sibling (in particular, no hard 33d0-before-822a edge: 822a names "the production path" abstractly and binds to whichever outbound applier survives 33d0's collapse by construction; against the current dual-path code the production batch path already carries the comment/link dispatch its round-trip exercises).
- Scope overlap ‚Äî BENIGN (benign-reading filter). 33d0 AC2 (OUTBOUND-only combined multi-sub-op UPDATE asserting each sub-op took effect = consolidation correctness), 8937 AC3 (assembled snapshot-entry CONTRACT through fetcher‚Üídiffer‚Üíapplier), and 822a AC1 (BIDIRECTIONAL inbound‚Üîoutbound comment/link/parent ROUND-TRIP, test_roundtrip.py) exercise the same production path from distinct angles ‚Äî directionality + round-trip vs single-direction application vs key/shape contract ‚Äî complementary, not duplicative, on distinct files. 822a explicitly scopes the schema/contract OUT (‚Üí8937).
- Ordering violation ‚Äî BENIGN. 822a's edited files are disjoint from the 33d0‚Üî2359 same-region outbound-applier sequencing cluster, so it is not entangled in that ordering hazard. The links/comments/issuelinks behavior 822a's round-trip relies on already exists in production, so no hard "33d0-before-822a" sequence is required for 822a's assertions to be meaningful; the only ordering edge (fixtures before the test that uses them) is captured by depends_on fe57.
- Consumer impact ‚Äî BENIGN. 822a AC3 keeps the hermetic round-trip in CI and any live variant opt-in/scheduled/documented (consistent with the epic's "live e2e is overkill / never flakes CI" posture and parent "no net increase in brittle tests"); AC4 asserts round-trip OUTCOMES, not whole-blob equality, imposing no brittle expectation on consumers.
- Residual references ‚Äî BENIGN. The `Phase3.verify-inbound-comments-gap ‚Ä¶ NOT-SYNCED` guard 822a converts exists verbatim in the repo (L1543-1547); no sibling deletes the probe scripts under scripts/jira-pressure-test, so its audit target is live. 33d0's deletion of the dead typed-leaf applier (`_apply_outbound_update`, reconcile.py L75 / typed_dispatch.py L56) strands nothing 822a references ‚Äî 822a drives the production batch path, not the typed leaf.

The roster-level parent anti-change-detector-guardrail matter is a G3 coverage question (and per the parent AC's explicit "(owned by story B)" clause is owned by 8937) ‚Äî not a G4 cross-child inconsistency, so not addressed here.

VERDICT for this pairing (parent √ó 822a): no G4 cross-child finding; all 7 interaction modes read benign under the high-confidence / benign-reading filter.
